description_long: This is a heavy-duty pipe wrench with a robust, dark metallic head and a thick, cylindrical wooden handle. The head features an adjustable jaw mechanism, with visible teeth for gripping pipes or fittings, and a knurled adjustment nut for changing the jaw width. The metal head shows signs of wear and oxidation, giving it a rugged, well-used appearance. The wooden handle is smooth, slightly tapered, and stained a deep brown, with a metallic collar connecting it to the head. The overall construction suggests durability and strength, suitable for plumbing or mechanical tasks in urban environments. The tool's utilitarian design is both functional and visually indicative of frequent use.
description: A rugged pipe wrench with a worn metal head and a thick, dark wooden handle, showing signs of heavy use. The adjustable jaw and knurled nut are prominent features.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wrench, highlighting the adjustable jaw, knurled nut, and the smooth wooden handle.
description_view_1: Front view focusing on the end of the wooden handle and the narrow profile of the wrench head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other side of the wrench head and the full handle, with the adjustment mechanism visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the wrench head and the rounded end of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Wooden straight handle, cylindrical, smooth with a slightly tapered end, stained dark brown, no visible grip enhancements.
attribute_head_type: Adjustable pipe wrench head, made of steel, with a toothed jaw and a knurled adjustment nut for changing jaw width.
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: Visible signs of wear, oxidation, and minor rust on the metal head; handle appears slightly worn but intact.
